title: Level 9.2
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:ㅤ}}__NOTOC__{{VoidedTheme|mono}} {{CSS|content= .fandom-community-header__image img { opacity: 0; } .fandom-community-header__image:before { content: ''; background: url(https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/backrooms/images/a/a2/Level_9.2_9.png) no-repeat; width: 305px; height: 300px; background-size: 95px; position: absolute; transition: 0s; } .page-content { overflow: visible; } }}
{{Background|file=Level 9.2 1.PNG}} = '''Level 9: "Darkened Suburbs"''' =
"Well, huh, we're getting closer to the place then, I assume?"


"Yep, just a few more minutes, I hope."



"You got those piles upon piles of notes with you, right? Just in case, we may have forgotten something."


"Relax! I got all of it with me, all stored safely inside a folder within my backpack. Entirely protected by none other than us."



"I-... You do realize I couldn't care less about the persistence of those old sets of notes? I am not protecting something with my life, as long as it's salvageable, by simply taking an image of it."


"Fair point. But well... we would kinda be screwed without them at the moment, wouldn't we?"



"Yeah, but you would also be screwed without me, so that's that."


"Sure I would... sure I would. Still, if possible, we should try to save both things."



"Yeah, yeah, I get it. The [[Level 9|Darkened Suburbs]] is gonna be much of a problem."


"You seem awfully confident in your ability here, don't you think? I don't believe cockiness is the best way to tread forward here."



"I know, I know, we shouldn't take this lightly, especially in areas we've never been to. Still, though, you gotta have some confidence in yourself at the very least.


"Let's just stop getting off-track here, not like we've walked this entire way for nothing."



"Yeah, you're right. Pull out the notes, I wanna know which direction we need to follow."


"So... according to these notes, we need to follow this street for a few more meters, then take a left, afterward a right, before taking a left again. The street apparently ends in a dead end, so we'll need to see from where we'll go from there."



"Sounds like a solid plan."





A cold, slowly corroding rose sits quietly next to the parchment.
Its florid light re-emerging from its hollow grave.



"So... here we are. The cul-de-sac is just like it was described by the notes itself. It is this place, isn't it?"


"Yes, it is. This exact street."



"What now though? What do we do after arriving at this place?"


"Well, the text at this portion is sorta slightly cryptic."



"Yeah, then tell me what it says already!"


"Alright, alright! Calm down. Here, I'll show you it."



A place where one must not shy of the night.

A place where even the unbeknown shall bloom in light.

A space where roseate breaks the nighttide.

Where fog absorbs the dark side.

Where the shrubs sit as tall as the trees.

Deep in the lurid purlieu, the short grassland stops, as if cut down by disease.



"This tells me barely anything."


"No, no, you need to look closer! Focus! Visualize the lines within your mind and make something out of them!"



"Yada-yada-yada, hell, I don't even know what I should focus on first."


"Hmm, take the second to last line, for example."



"'Where the shrubs sit as tall as the trees.' So as in... as in a forest maybe?"


"Exactly, that's the same thing I was thinking. So if we just track down something like a forest, maybe we'll actually reach it!"



"I'm pretty sure I saw an overgrown backyard like a few streets away from here. Could it possibly be, or maybe even lead to the place we're trying to find?"


"Wait, you did!? Yes, yes, of course, it may lead to the, place we're trying to locate. Let's head there... right now!"



The backyard goes on and on, slowly removing every and all resemblance of human interference.
The night slowly faded into the fuchsia dusk.
{{Background|file=Level 9.2 2.PNG}}




As so, the night finally diminishes into obscurity. The darkness is evanescent into roseate. No sun rising, no moon setting, an ever-blank sky. An ever-growing mist reaching out its arms and covering the world with its gloves. A ghastly gloom that looms over the now more vivid forest. Unsure as if to provide protection or to warn.


The forest is dead. Not a single chirp, trill, or other natural sound piercing the silence. As if the fog had consumed every organism, every species, every little inch of bacteria. Entirely clean. Not even the trees dared to hush among themselves, standing windless there. Like a frozen statue, an inanimate object, nothing more, nothing less. It was simply there, no reason to persist when asphyxiated by the rose haze. Differentiating colors from one another turned into difficult mind games, everything coated in a strong pinkish hue.


Nature being too oversaturated, it was as if looking at a megacosm stronger than itself, stronger than meant to be. Painted in obscenity and beauty, more than what we humans could ever even imagine seeing back [[The Frontrooms|Home]]. It eradicates what one would see under their definitions, and blow the concept out of fair proportions. It does not feel human, it does not give off the atmosphere nature would, only a mimicry of resemblance. It removes the word 'forest' from earthly definitions that you cling to so dearly.


Sight forward is limited. Never know what to expect in the thick, hazy fog. It is a struggle to even manage to truly gaze at the grass a few meters in front of oneself. The unknown stares down my every move, never to be sure that I am fully safe. An unsettling feeling of being watched by someone, by something through the dense morning mist. Traversing this forest is as reliable as playing a guessing game, never knowing where you might descend or end up.


Yet admiring nor fearing the roseate forest, there was no denying that the only option there was, was to continue. Whether that may lead to somewhere more sustainable or will lead me right back here, is not known. But there is no other way to find out other than to continue. Hopping over inclines and declines, over small cliffs, and under shadowy timber. Never knowing if I've made progress or not, never knowing if I walked in circles, the ever-changing yet monotone landscape remaining vivid and somehow stale at the same time.































































Yet, it doesn't remain monotone, as the trees diminish and suburbs arise.



































= '''Level 9.2 - "Cul-De-Sac Chaos"''' =
{{Background|file=Level 9.2 3.PNG}} {{Class Variable|class=Undetermined|hexagon=? |safety=Undetermined Properties |security=Stability Indeterminable |entity=Presence of Entities Undetermined |color-h=266 |color-s=100% |color-l=43%}} '''Level 9.2''', more commonly referred to as "Cul-De-Sac Chaos" is a sub-level between [[Level 9|The Bumper Crop]]. Due to its relatively hidden nature, only scarce information is known about this level.
Roads
{{Underline|fff}} {{Blurimage|file=Level 9.2 10.png|caption=Absurd and its ilk.}} The layout of the roads within this level is chaotic. Almost every street ends in a cul-de-sac, bestowing the name of '''Level 9.2'''. Roads lead the way carelessly, sometimes even going through entire buildings. Navigating or even mapping this suburban neighborhood can be difficult, as it's a mess to find out how the formations of this level truly work, as there isn't even a resemblance of organized road patterns. This, combined with the already limiting mist, makes these suburbans nigh impossible to traverse effectively. The streets connecting the vacant neighborhoods are often unnaturally raised off the ground, being more an organized pile of asphalt than truly embedded into the ground. These streets also regularly appear to be at least somewhat moist, presumably from the roseate fog, to a point where one may easily slip and fall if not careful. Streetlights also rarely line the sides of these roads, stuck in a constant state of flickering off and on again, never seeming to rest. They act in a gauche manner, with no pattern nor consistency between each flick, seemingly at completely random intervals. Somehow still flickering even after not even remotely being connected to any form of electricity.
Entrances
{{Underline|fff}} *The main and most common entrance to this level, is to find a large forest at the end of a cul-de-sac in [[Level 9|Polluted Pools]] may occasionally lead here.
The Bumper Crop]], which may lead one exactly there. *Entering a car, closely resembling [[The Mustang|Hilbert's Motel]]. *No-clipping through a flower here may lead one to [[Level 70|Under Construction]].
{{Redirect|Prev=9.1|Next=9.31}}

An ancient TV flickers through static, an old operating system greeting the nobody.
|hide=
The static diminishes.
|content={{Background|file=Level 9.2 8.PNG}}

[[File:computer screen thingy.png|3000px]]
[[File:1130-2.gif|3000px]]
[[File:MS-DOS PROMPT.png|thumb|link=|24x24px|left]] '''Memento''' [[File:MS-DOS OPTIONS.png|thumb|link=|256x256px]]
[[File:MS-DOS CUSTOM.png|thumb|link=|center|750x750px]]

'''Tape #921''' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} Does this- oh my God... Yes! It does work! Let's go- alright, let's see here... Okay, okay, sorry if I seem a bit through the weather at the moment, I'm just a bit shocked that it actually worked. I didn't really expect it to, but oh well, it's for the better! Let me just give you a small and short introduction to who I am... if anybody will ever find this tape, but eh that's out of my control really. Hi, I'm Liam, possibly Liam Hiroto if you wanna really be formal about it. 20 years old, heritage from Japan, 5-foot nin- I mean... 6-foot tall, and uhmmm that's about it from what I can think of. Not a whole lot to say about my life, really. I've been in this different dimension, usually called the "Backrooms" or whatever, for quite a while now. I'll probably leave this tape here where I am currently recording it from but, just in case it does get moved by whomever, I'll mention where I currently reside. I'm currently in this desolate suburban neighborhood, with this rose... pinkish fog widespread on the outside. It's relatively bearable in here, although this house does give me the heebie-jeebies at least somewhat. Oh well, not like there is a better place to reside in currently. I could've been still stuck in that damned forest, which is honestly a whole other can of worms to unpack. That's a problem for future me, though! My hope is still clenching on the idea that there is an exit within the suburban, there must be, right? I do still have some supplies left, not a lot but some. Some cans of conserved food, a few bottles of water, some of which is even [[Object 1|of almond kind]], so that's pretty nifty I would say. I'm not an expert when it comes to foodstuffs but, I'd say that'll last me a couple of weeks, maybe 2 or 3 if I stay resourceful. There should hopefully be more than enough time to get out of here, hopefully to a place that's less... empty.









'''Tape #922''' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} There... there we go! It works again. Good God, trying to get these tape recorders to actually work is harder than it looks. I swear it was easier the last... then again, I'm not really an expert with technology, so what do I know. Uh anyway, got a bit carried off track there, let's get back to the point. I ventured on a small exploration mission a few hours ago into the suburbans of this level, and goddamn the layout of these suburbans is... quite interesting... actually, there's no reason to hold back: they're pretty fucking terrible, ain't they? It's like trying to find a nail in a haystack, wait... needle! It was a needle, for fuck's sake. Either way, I explored the neighborhood for quite a long interval of time. There was nothing really sticking out of the ordinary, just a bunch of suburban homes, nothing more nor less. I'm thinking about exploring further, just taking a straight route through the suburbia, to not get lost on my way back. See what else this area has to offer! Seriously, I hope something breaks the dullness of this place soon, or else I'll end up being moonstruck... or more like fogstruck. Get it? Because of the mist- anyways moving on from blabbering nonsense. I unfortunately did not find anything to support my search for supplies. It's weird how deadly quiet this place is, like even the [[Level 0|ever-ridden rooms with hum-buzz]] had more life than this. But, I digress, if I want a way out of here, then I need to focus, and not distract myself with the allurement of itself. I need to move on and continue my venture into the depths here, till I find something of use, there must be, right?









'''Tape #923''' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} And... and... Come on! There! There we go, nice, so... I have retraced my steps and bounced back to my humble little shelter. I... I didn't find much of interest... pfff who am I kidding? I found jack shit, yeah that's right, nothing! Absolutely nothing that could bring me closer! Do you wanna know the best part? It took me multiple days, entire days of walking! For what? The knowledge of never making such a critical decision in my life ever again. So my plan was, after I left the last tape, to move out after tomorrow morning... whatever I had considered tomorrow morning, not like day and night really exist in this roseate hellscape of a place. Kinda how I already mentioned, walk in a straight line through the suburbs, in order to ferret out multeity. Just... at the very least something to break the ice, but no! Nothing. Oh, wow, who could've guessed? While I did get something, it for sure as hell wasn't the thing I was desperately searching for. I winded up in this large neighborhood with towering Victorian homes and virtually everything with it. But still, even after getting the change from monotony that I so gravely wanted, it still brought me nothing. It's a good-for-nothing-type situation. Not even the dark and dull manors could provide at the very least anything. Possibly I am getting too desperate to notice my own stoop, possibly I have overlooked something, missed something, anything. But, I am unsure, it's totally possible that I did miss something, yet I also cannot go back and check, it would be too expensive. My sheer existence hanging on a loosely tied end, waiting to break at any moment. Any and all my decisions could be between life and death, yet I don't know if there even is a way to escape that fate, there must be, right?









'''Tape #924''' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} Come on already! I swear to God, this fuck-... Never mind, there we go. Hey, it is me, Liam, again, oh boy sure was a surprise, wasn't it? I'll keep this enumeration pithy. I am slowly losing the battle against time itself, backed up to the creek and at the end of the rope, wondering if I can win against lastingness. Or if I'll give up my ghost before I'll see the end of the tunnel. Lamentably, I'm penchant towards a certain standpoint, one of melancholy. By all means, it's not guaranteed yet, my fate is not yet sealed, however, one may emphasize on the word "yet", and dignify it by underlining it for all I could care. I'm just bombarding the walls with thoughts to see what sticks, if any. I don't even know if these words, these locutions, will reach anybody at all. Maybe I am just truly all by myself. Wholly isolated. I am unsure of how long I have left here, I've used up most of my supplies, and only the reserves are left. I'll give myself about 1 or maybe 2 days spent not starving. I don't want to be entirely ill-fated, but I don't have much more hope, much more castles in the air. I'll venture into the depths of the forest one last time, either now or never. It's the only choice I have left. What else am I supposed to do? I just have to believe that there must be a way to break out, there must be, right?









'''Tape #925''' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} Here I am, oh Holy Spirit, is this what you would've wanted? Don't break away, you haven't departed! Not yet. I'm not afraid of your empty threats! My last breath will be spent in spite. Even if it may not be entirely right. On one half, I'm not sure if I walked into my own grave. Whoever it may save. Yet, what else was I supposed to do? Even if I somewhat knew. Knowledge of my forthcoming providence. Should've known where my venture would lead me, at the start, at the provenance. Was I a fool? Yes, for believing it's fecund, full of jewel. Should I have thought twice before entering an area I didn't know? I should have a long time ago. I'd hand myself a total of... 2, maybe 3 hours? Yet, my sentiment's already depraved, all my towers crumble down under the flowers. I lay in the field, exhausted. All of my progress halted. Self-reflection on the last I still own. Trees all around me, yet I remain all alone. Should I shift the blame towards somebody else? Am I truly the one that broke the ice? Exclaim that it isn't my fault, no guilt. But also no way to rebuild. You absolutely ruined me! Even something as explicable as that, would it ever make me free? Will I, ever be the same? Of course not, although I could proclaim. What point is there to pretend? When, even I, will soon meet my end. My last steps spent in agony, of sheer willpower, of sheer will-force. I've already decided, set in stone, as that, is my course. No more striving power, no more striving capability. I want it to come to an end so badly, but that is not in my ability. The only thing keeping me going is the fear of death. Barely with the ability to think of fear, barely enough strength. But I carry on, as it's the only thing that matters. Nothing else needs to end up shattered.









As I make my way through the crowd of verdure, through the meadows and the gallows, I notice the grass turning xanthous. The blossom curling, the viridian retreating, the grassland slowly resembling nothing like it was before, not even in the slightest monantheous. The trees return to their roots. Sat deep below my boots. Yet something was not right. In the blink of an eye, something horrid emerged an abominable sight.
[[File:Level 9.2 7.png|center|link=|500x500px]]
''From the depths of hell, something arose. No escape now, is there?''
}}
